prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "胫骨前肌抬脚" (tibialis anterior raise (seated)) exercise, side view from the right, focused on the lower leg and foot. Person posture: Athletic person seated on a chair, right leg extended forward with the right heel resting on the floor (knee almost fully extended, slight bend). The right foot is FULLY DORSIFLEXED — the toes and forefoot are pulled UP toward the shin as far as possible, creating a sharp angle at the ankle. The contracted tibialis anterior muscle bulges visibly along the front-outer side of the right shin (lateral aspect of the tibia). Left leg is bent and resting normally. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: tibialis anterior (front-outer side of the shin). Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: extensor digitorum longus (toe extensors). Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "胫骨前肌" pointing to the tibialis anterior; "趾长伸肌" pointing to the toe extensor muscles; "胫骨" pointing to the tibia bone. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
